data.title: Marex to acquire UK equity market maker Winterflood Securities
data.description:
Marex Group (NASDAQ:MRX) has agreed to acquire UK equity market maker Winterflood Securities (Winterflood) from Close Brothers for about £103.9 million in cash, which represents a premium of £15 million.

The acquisition is expected to enhance Marex’s existing UK cash equities business, consistent with its strategy to bring new clients and new capabilities onto its platform and diversify earnings.

"This deal is consistent with our strict financial criteria, and we see opportunities to materially improve Winterflood’s profitability and pay back its premium within two to three years," Ian Lowitt, Marex Group Chief Executive Officer said.

It is also expected to add a substantial distribution offering servicing the UK institutional community, in particular asset and wealth management companies, with the potential to deepen these relationships by offering access to a broader range of Marex’s products from across its platform.

The deal is subject to regulatory approval and is expected to close in early 2026.

data.title: NatWest reports 1H results
data.description:
• NatWest press release (NYSE:NWG): 1H reported an attributable profit for the period of £1,854 million and a Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) ratio of 11.3% for NWB Plc.
  • Total income increased by £497 million, or 8%, to £6,360 million compared with £5,863 million in H1 2024.  
  • Net interest income increased by £602 million, or 15%, to £4,543 million, compared with £3,941 million in H1 2024, as a result of deposit margin expansion, balance sheet growth and higher structural hedge income.
  • Net loans to customers increased by £4.2 billion to £336.2 billion during the period, including personal loans and credit card balances acquired from Sainsbury's Bank, which were £2.2 billion at 30 June 2025.

data.title: UK retail sales rise lower than expected in June
data.description:
• UK retail sales rose 0.9% M/M in June 2025, rebounding from a revised 2.8% drop in the previous month but missing market expectations of a 1.2% gain. It marked the fourth increase so far this year.
  • On a yearly basis, retail sales increased 1.7%, swinging from a revised 1.1% drop in May, though slightly below forecasts of a 1.8% rise.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Elon Musk 'sorry' after Starlink suffers rare outage
data.description:
Musk’s satellite internet network, Starlink (STRLK) experienced a rare and massive global outage on Thursday, July 24, due to an internal software failure that took users offline. 

"Starlink is currently in a network outage, and we are actively implementing a solution," the company said in a brief statement posted to X.

Elon Musk followed up with a personal apology, writing, “Sorry for the outage. SpaceX will remedy root cause to ensure it doesn’t happen again,” he wrote on platform X.

The outage was a rare hiccup in Starlink’s (STRLK) operational history and lasted approximately 2.5 hours for most affected customers. Outage tracking platforms such as Downdetector recorded over 60,000 reports globally. 

Doug Madory, Director of Internet Analysis at Kentik, said that Starlink's network went through what he described as a "total outage" at around 3:13 PM ET, according to a Reuters report."This is likely the longest outage ever for Starlink, at least while it became a major service provider," Madory added.

In recent months, SpaceX (SPACE) has concentrated largely on modernizing its network to meet requests for increased speed and bandwidth as Starlink's (STRLK) user base has grown.

data.title: Samsung in talks to expand smartphone AI beyond Google’s Gemini - report
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) is in talks with the likes of OpenAI and Perplexity AI to integrate more AI services in upcoming Galaxy devices, seeking a wider range of offerings beyond Google’s (NASDAQ:GOOG) Gemini, Bloomberg reported.

South Korea’s largest company aims to give customers more options on its upcoming Galaxy S26 smartphone line next year, according to Choi Won-Joon, president and chief operating officer of its mobile division.

“We are talking to multiple vendors,” Choi said in an interview at Samsung’s headquarters in Suwon. “As long as these AI agents are competitive and can provide the best user experiences, we are open to any AI agent out there.”

Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) was nearing a deal to invest in Nvidia-backed (NVDA) Perplexity AI and integrate its app and assistant, Bloomberg reported in June.

Like its arch-rival Apple (AAPL), the Galaxy phone maker is aiming to enhance its AI capabilities by integrating top-tier solutions from external providers. Samsung’s current lineup features Alphabet's (GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) AI model, like much of the rest of the field of Android devices.

data.title: Trump’s Scotland trip sparks UK push to resolve steel tariff issue, digital tax remains a sticking point
data.description:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to press U.S. President Donald Trump to finalize a trade agreement that would cut tariffs on UK steel and Scotch whisky imports, the Financial Times.

Starmer will meet Trump on Monday during the president’s visit to Scotland, where he’s scheduled to tour his golf properties in Turnberry and Aberdeen.

The leaders will discuss strengthening US-UK trade ties and refining a deal that took effect on June 30, which established a 10% baseline tariff on UK goods entering the U.S. while providing certain quotas and exemptions for autos and aerospace.

However, key elements remain unresolved, including the pledge to remove a 25% tariff on UK steel and aluminum - a lower rate compared to the 50% duty applied to other countries. For the tariff relief to take full effect, the UK must demonstrate that its steel imports are "melted and poured" domestically and not sourced from China.

Digital services tax disputes that affect U.S. tech companies [(GOOG), (GOOGL), (AMZN), (META), (AAPL)] also remain a point of contention, with Washington wanting them to be removed.

Trump’s visit to Scotland precedes a formal state visit scheduled for September 17–19, the White House confirmed. The president, accompanied by First Lady Melania Trump, expressed that he is “honored” and looking forward to meeting King Charles and Queen Camilla at Windsor Castle this fall.

Here is a list of ETFs to track the broader U.S. market: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

And here are ETFs to track UK market: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (GBP:USD)

data.title: Hong Kong-based OSL Group secures $300M in equity financing to expand crypto business
data.description:
Hong Kong's OSL Group (OTCPK:BCTCF) has secured $300 million in equity financing, the largest publicly disclosed equity raise in Asia's digital asset sector to date, it said on Friday.

Despite a 10% drop in share price following the announcement, OSL’s stock has surged nearly 120% year-to-date, signaling strong investor confidence driven by regulatory clarity and growing institutional demand.

The deal comes days before Hong Kong's stablecoin bill takes effect on August 1.

This landmark financing underscores strong market confidence in OSL's digital asset business model and long-term growth trajectory while laying a solid foundation for the Group's next phase of international expansion, the company said.

The funds will be used to expand regulated stablecoin infrastructure, secure licenses in new jurisdictions, and build a compliant digital payments network.

data.title: China's BYD looks to assemble EVs in Pakistan from 2026
data.description:
• Chinese EV giant BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) (OTCPK:BYDDY) is looking to roll out its first car assembled in Pakistan by July or August 2026 to capture growing demand in the region for electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles, a company executive told Reuters.
  • BYD, the world's top EV maker, has been expanding rapidly outside its home market, where it is in a strong price war.
  • The plant has been under construction since April near Karachi in a partnership between BYD and Mega Motor Company, a subsidiary of Pakistani utility Hub Power, the report said.
  • It would initially have the capacity to produce 25,000 units a year on a double shift.
  • The EV maker also plans to start manufacturing cars earlier than expected at a new plant in Turkey, where labor costs are lower, as per earlier reports.

data.title: Volkswagen slashes full-year outlook, Q2 profit plunges amid hefty US tariffs
data.description:
• Volkswagen press release (OTCPK:VWAGY) has lowered its full-year guidance and reported a sharp drop in second-quarter profit, as the auto giant navigates the disruptive impact of U.S. tariffs.
  • For the second quarter, the company recorded 2.26 million vehicle sales. Production, however, totaled 2.33 million units, a 0.6% decrease from the previous year.
  • The operating profit of 3.83 billion euros ($4.49 billion) for the three months through June, down 29% from 5.4 billion euros a year ago.
  • Revenue of €80.81B (-3.0% Y/Y).
  • The automotive giant is navigating a challenging global trade environment, with the tariffs, notably a 27.5% levy on certain vehicles, significantly impacting its North American operations and overall profitability.
  • 4.36 million vehicle sales in H1 2025, slightly above H1 2024 (4.34 million vehicles).
  • Outlook for the year 2025 as of July 25, 2025: 
  • The Volkswagen Group expects sales revenue to be in line with the previous year’s figure (previously: increase of up to 5 percent). The Group’s operating return on sales is expected to range between 4.0 and 5.0 percent (previously: 5.5 to 6.5 percent).
  • In the Automotive Division, the Volkswagen Group continues to expect an investment ratio between 12 and 13 percent in 2025. Automotive net cash flow for 2025 is expected to be between EUR 1 and EUR 3 billion (previously: EUR 2 to EUR 5 billion). This includes cash outflows for investments for the future as well as for restructuring measures. Net liquidity in the Automotive Division in 2025 is expected to be between EUR 31 and EUR 33 billion (previously: EUR 34 to EUR 37 billion).
  • The Group continues to pursue its objective of maintaining a solid financing and liquidity policy. At the lower end of the forecast ranges for operating result, net cash flow and net liquidity, it is assumed that in particular the current US import tariffs of 27.5 percent will continue to apply in the second half of 2025; at the upper end, it is assumed that these tariffs will be reduced to 10 percent.

data.title: Asia stocks fall as markets brace for Trump tariff deadline, central bank meetings
data.description:
Asian shares pulled back on Friday, with Japanese markets notably dipping from a recent record, as investors chose to secure profits. This comes ahead of a pivotal week featuring a deadline for U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs and multiple central bank meetings.

Gold prices fell to around $3,360 per ounce on Friday, marking a third consecutive session of losses, as concerns over tariffs continued to ease.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 0.87% to below 41,700, while the Topix Index dropped 0.6% to 2,960 on Friday, as investors booked profits following a sharp rally that pushed both indexes to new highs. The Japanese yen slipped to around 147.5 per dollar on Friday, marking its second consecutive day of losses, as markets digested the implications of the new US-Japan trade agreement.

Japan’s index of coincident economic indicators—which tracks key data such as factory output, employment, and retail sales—stood at 116.0 in May 2025, slightly above the flash estimate of 115.9 but unchanged from April's reading.

Japan’s leading economic index—which reflects the outlook for the coming months based on indicators such as job offers and consumer sentiment—was revised down to 104.8 in May 2025 from a preliminary estimate of 105.3. 

On the data front, Tokyo’s core inflation in July came in below expectations but remained well above the Bank of Japan’s 2% target, reinforcing market expectations for another rate hike this year. 

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.23% to below 3,600, while the Shenzhen Component edged down 0.1% to 11,180 on Friday, as investors turned cautious ahead of further US-China trade negotiations next week, and the offshore yuan weakened to around 7.15 against the US dollar on Friday, retreating from an over eight-month high reached in the previous session.

China on Thursday released a draft amendment to its pricing law to address excessive competition and price wars, amid ongoing deflationary pressures. The proposed changes aim to curb practices like selling below cost to eliminate competitors or monopolize markets, except in cases of legitimate discounts such as for seasonal or overstocked goods. 

Investor attention is also focused on China’s industrial profits data due this weekend.

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.82% to 25,410 on Friday morning, down for the first time in six sessions, as traders took profits after markets hit a near 4-year top earlier in the week.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.75% to 81,827 on Friday, extending losses from the previous session and hitting its lowest level since mid-June.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.50% dropped 0.5% to 8,664 in morning trade on Friday, extending losses from the previous session, with nearly all sectors in the red. The Australian dollar fell to around $0.658 on Friday, extending losses from the previous session, as investors remained cautious ahead of key inflation data due next week. 

Meanwhile, investors are closely watching developments in US–Australia trade relations. In the latest development, Australia has agreed to ease restrictions on US beef imports—a decision that follows tensions over previous trade limitations.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended mixed, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq 100 closed at fresh record highs on Thursday, rising 0.1% and 0.2% respectively, supported by strong earnings from Alphabet that bolstered investor confidence in AI investments. 

U.S. stock futures edged higher on Friday after the major averages ended mixed in the previous session: Dow +0.24%; S&P 500 +0.23%; Nasdaq +0.18%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).
